title: Lithocardium grandiflorum
taxonomy: Lithocardium grandiflorum is classified in the plant family Gesneriaceae. This family consists of tropical flowering plants commonly known as the African violets or the Gesneriad family. The Gesneriaceae family belongs to the order Lamiales in the angiosperm plant classification. The order Lamiales is a part of the clade Asterids, which is one of the two main groups of flowering plants. The clade Asterids is further classified under the larger group of flowering plants known as the Angiosperms.
uses: Lithocardium grandiflorum is known to have various traditional medicinal uses. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, as it is believed to have antipyretic properties. It is also used as an antidote for certain types of poisoning, such as that caused by aconitine. Additionally, the plant is said to have diuretic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of edema and other conditions related to fluid retention. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these uses.
